column7: message

1) ok i worded the point poorly it should have been a mixture of Africans and Asians anyone but whites having the majority of characters is unrealistic maybe a couple. 

2)Im basing it on the fact that Africa has the highest fertility rate in the world.  Star trek is about the United earth not united States.

>It really breaks my immersion and pisses me off. For example i was watching star trek reboot and everyone was white thats so stupid and unrealistic it should have been all cast black and one white in place of uhura. 

Why?  Black population is not really rising as a percentage of the population.  It is historically pretty flat for the last century.  It certainly isnt trending as you seem to think.

https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Historical_racial_and_ethnic_demographics_of_the_United_States

@grim-cataract I get where you're coming from, but let's be real, a show set in the future shouldn't just be about demographics. It's supposed to inspire and unite, not just reflect current stats. And @complicated-binary, you got some facts there, but come on, can't we dream a bit bigger than just mirroring today's numbers? Star Trek is about possibilities, right?

column7:
Well the difference in how people view Trump’s behavior is largely due to Trump changing his behavior. His bellicosity and name calling is counter productive and could have led to a war. That is as true now as it was back then. No one thought it was helpful to have him running around saying there was no point to diplomacy.

Now, Trump is calling Kim [honorable](https://www.google.com/amp/s/amp.cnn.com/cnn/2018/04/24/politics/trump-kim-jong-un-honorable/index.html), praising him as a strong leader, and agreeing to meet with him. Of course the reactions are going to be different, and pretending it’s just people wanting to shit on Trump regardless is disingenuous. The world is doing a 180 because Trump himself did.

Additionally,  the world (China specifically) started cracking down harder on NK because they were laughing missiles at a greater frequency, and with less regard for their immediate neighbors.  That part had little to do with Trump or the US.

Lastly, while I think it’s fair to give Trump a small amount of credit just for being the guy in office at the time, I think we should wait to see how this all plays out  before we start handing out awards to anyone. If this just means a NK with nukes and no sanctions, I don’t think we’ve really solved the problem.

>Well the difference in how people view Trump’s behavior is largely due to Trump changing his behavior. His bellicosity and name calling is counter productive and could have led to a war. That is as true now as it was back then. No one thought it was helpful to have him running around saying there was no point to diplomacy.

I've already explained how that's not the case.  You still haven't actually addressed the logic of what he's doing.

>Now, Trump is calling Kim honorable, praising him as a strong leader, and agreeing to meet with him. 

Gee, it's almost like trump was trying to pressure kim before, and now that Kim is signalling he's willing to talk, Trump is rewarding him!  Maybe there's even some sort of strategy!

>Of course the reactions are going to be different, and pretending it’s just people wanting to shit on Trump regardless is disingenuous. The world is doing a 180 because Trump himself did.

No, he didn't. But even if trump did, you are claiming that trump literally is not capable of doing what he is currently doing!  And you see no hypocrisy in this!

>Lastly, while I think it’s fair to give Trump a small amount of credit just for being the guy in office at the time, I think we should wait to see how this all plays out before we start handing out awards to anyone. If this just means a NK with nukes and no sanctions, I don’t think we’ve really solved the problem.

I absolutely agree.  The north is always willing to talk, them saying so again means nothing.  But that's not the point here, the point is your total inability to conceive of the possibility that trump might be pursuing a fairly obvious strategy in north korea.  A week ago, if I had asked you "if kim said he was willing make peace, do you think trump would keep insulting him?" there is no doubt you'd have said yes.  Your theory of how the world works failed to predict what has happened.  I humbly suggest that you re-think your theory and fit it to the facts, not stick to it and twist the facts to fit.

I guess it's hard to tell what's really going on with international politics. Trump's approach seems to have changed, but whether it's a strategy or just a shift in stance is tough to say. Either way, let's hope it leads to something good for everyone involved.

column7:
> Palestine decided to go to war against them

When was that?

> So the ball is in Palestine's court to decide what they want to do; peace with Israel or continue to fight/lose.

I don't think that's fair. Israel continues to expand settlements in the West Bank--that's one of the main points of contention. It's not like that stops if they stop resisting. (And how could they effectively resist anyway? One side controls nearly all of the resources.)

Well... Israel won that right through might when Palestine decided to go to war against them. Israel ain't going to lay down it's weapons since every Arab nation hates them. So the ball is in Palestine's court to decide what they want to do; peace with Israel or continue to fight/lose.

And Palestine itself is at odds within itself; Gaza vs West Bank. So that shit needs to get straightened out before any sort of compromise can be reached. The people are paying for the mistakes of their leadership is making.

@jovial-sort "Israel won that right through might" sounds a lot like might makes right. And who exactly "decided to go to war"? The narrative is a bit more complex than one side just deciding to attack the other. Also, saying it’s up to Palestine to decide when Israel continues to build settlements feels like asking someone to agree to terms while you're still taking their land. Seems pretty one-sided to me.
"@jovial-sort, please avoid language that could escalate tensions. Let's keep the discussion respectful and focused on constructive dialogue."
